file_title: ZEV Mandate Compliance: Supply Chain Investments
introduction: In response to Canada's revised ZEV mandate, understanding and implementing updated compliance documentation is crucial for Elexion Automotive. This presentation will discuss how we can effectively verify and record supply-chain investment credits by 2026.

subsections.heading: Overview of Canada’s Revised ZEV Mandate
subsections.content:
• In order to align with Canada's updated ZEV mandate, Elexion Automotive needs to revamp its compliance documentation framework.
• A critical aspect of this transition involves ensuring our compliance staff are adequately trained on the revised verification protocols.
• The training scope is expected to encompass approximately 55 of our compliance personnel.
• These personnel will collectively require around 1,200 hours of training to fully comprehend the new protocols.
• By Q2 2026, we need to have completed this training to avoid creating audit gaps.
• The training timeline, in turn, will impact our budget and scheduling decisions for the fiscal year 2026.
• Therefore, it is crucial that we structure our compliance documentation framework in a manner that allows sufficient time for staff training.
• This strategic investment in our people readiness is key to our successful adaptation to the revised ZEV mandate.
subsections.justification: This content outlines the necessary steps and timeline for Elexion Automotive to update its compliance documentation framework under Canada’s revised ZEV mandate.

subsections.heading: Current Compliance Documentation Framework
subsections.content:
• Elexion Automotive uses a GRC software tool for managing documentation
• The current documentation process involves three departments: procurement, legal, and finance
• The documentation framework was last updated in 2018
• Quarterly audits are conducted to ensure the accuracy of the documentation
• The existing framework's storage system is cloud-based for easy access
• All documents in the framework are encrypted to ensure security
subsections.justification: This slide outlines the current documentation framework without suggesting how it should be updated.

subsections.heading: Proposed Changes to Documentation Framework
subsections.content:
• Introducing a new process for document verification that includes AI technology
• Integration of blockchain technology for secure and transparent transactions
• Moving towards a paperless documentation system by 2025
• Implementing a new training program for employees to adapt to the changes
• Upgrading the GRC software tool to a newer version for better performance
• Implementing a two-factor authentication system for accessing documents
subsections.justification: The slide discusses potential changes to the documentation framework but doesn't relate to the supply-chain investment credits under Canada's ZEV mandate.

conclusion: By aligning our compliance documentation framework with the updated ZEV mandate, Elexion Automotive can ensure proper recording of supply-chain investment credits. Moving forward, let's strategize on implementing these proposed changes for a smooth transition by 2026.
